steps.title: Spot the contrast in fame
steps.content: The first sentence presents William Still as famous during his lifetime. The next sentence says that despite this fame, he is discussed far less today than other prominent abolitionists.

steps.title: Choose the contrast signal
steps.content: Choice A introduces an example, but the second sentence is not an example of Still's work. Choice C introduces a specific elaboration, not an opposition. Choice D signals similarity, while the sentences contrast past fame with present obscurity. The transition must mark opposition. The answer is B.
